goal: Complete RR-11: LOG_LEVEL env var, unit tests for _redact_secrets, and THREAT_MODEL.md update.

tasks.name: Add LOG_LEVEL env var support to server.py
tasks.action: In setuplogging.py (or server.py if setuplogging handles it), read LOG_LEVEL from env (default 'INFO') and configure the root logger level accordingly. If setuplogging.py already exists and sets up logging, add LOG_LEVEL there. Otherwise add it to server.py before the logger is used. The env var should accept standard Python level names (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR).
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. Manually confirm server.py or setuplogging.py reads LOG_LEVEL env var.
tasks.done: Server reads LOG_LEVEL env var and configures logging level at startup. Default remains INFO.

tasks.name: Add unit tests for _redact_secrets and log-level changes
tasks.action: In test_server.py, add a TestRedactSecrets class with tests covering: (1) known token is redacted, (2) non-string input returned unchanged, (3) empty _SECRET_TOKENS list returns text unchanged, (4) multiple tokens all redacted. Also add a TestLogLevel test (or extend existing) that verifies the LOG_LEVEL env var is respected by setuplogging/server init.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. New test class visible in output.
tasks.done: test_server.py contains TestRedactSecrets with ≥4 test cases covering the _redact_secrets function.

tasks.name: Update THREAT_MODEL.md to mark RR-11 resolved
tasks.action: In THREAT_MODEL.md: (1) Find the RR-11 section and update its title to strikethrough and add RESOLVED with today's date (2026-03-28). (2) Update the status description to note that _redact_secrets() redacts known tokens from all log lines, stdout/stderr moved to DEBUG, LOG_LEVEL env var added. (3) In Section 9 priority table, update the RR-11 row to mark it done with date and brief summary.
tasks.verify: grep THREAT_MODEL.md for RR-11 shows resolved status. run_tests passes.
tasks.done: THREAT_MODEL.md marks RR-11 as resolved with implementation summary and date.
